title: Mangifera zeylanica
intro: Mangifera zeylanica, also known as the Ceylon olive tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Anacardiaceae. The tree is valued for its medicinal properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mangifera zeylanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Mangifera zeylanica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It belongs to the genus Mangifera and is a close relative of the more commonly known mango tree, Mangifera indica. The exact classification of Mangifera zeylanica is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Rosids, order - Sapindales, family - Anacardiaceae, genus - Mangifera, species - M. zeylanica. Mangifera zeylanica is native to Sri Lanka, where it grows as a small to medium-sized tree. This species is often considered to be a lesser-known variety of mango, with similarities in its fruit and leaves to the more widely cultivated mango tree.
uses: Mangifera zeylanica, commonly known as the Sri Lankan or East Indian mango, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's bark, leaves, and seeds are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant's bark is used to treat diarrhea and dysentery due to its astringent properties. The leaves of Mangifera zeylanica are also used as a poultice to treat wounds and reduce inflammation.
benefits: Mangifera zeylanica is a plant species that has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and it is known to have several health benefits. The fruit of the plant, also known as the Ceylon olive or mango, is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against infections and reduce inflammation in the body. The plant is also said to have antioxidant and anti-aging properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and promote overall health and well-being. Additionally, the leaves and bark of the plant are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
properties: Mangifera zeylanica, also known as the Ceylon olive, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its reported therapeutic properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may be beneficial in treating various health conditions. Its extracts are believed to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which could help in protecting against oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antidiabetic and antihypertensive properties, which may be useful in managing diabetes and hypertension. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Mangifera zeylanica for therapeutic purposes.
constituents: Mangifera zeylanica is known to contain various active constituents, which include flavonoids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The fruit's peel is particularly rich in flavonoids, while the leaves contain a significant amount of phenolic compounds. The presence of these active constituents contributes to the plant's traditional uses in medicine, such as treating fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its bioactive properties.
parts: Mangifera zeylanica, also known as the Ceylon mango or Sri Lankan mango, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
